---
title: "Semantic Pages, Context Cells, and Certificates"
chapter_id: "semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "vcm_public"
  - "verification_bandwidth"
  - "spinoza"
  - "context_engineer"
  - "vcm_editable"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Semantic Pages, Context Cells, and Certificates

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `vcm_public`, `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`, `vcm_editable` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Compiled context needs typed semantic units and proof-carrying summaries that preserve provenance and loss contracts.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Summaries can look authoritative while dropping source bindings, omissions, authority ceilings, or allowed-use limits.

## Core Claim

[semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] VCM should represent context as typed semantic pages or cells with evidence-carrying representation certificates.

## Mechanism

- Classify pages as constraints, claims, decisions, corrections, events, artifacts, and other typed cells.
- Attach certificates with source bindings, omissions, validity, authority ceilings, and permitted uses.
- Use task-relative representation graphs instead of one linear compression ladder.

## Interfaces

- Spinoza consumes claim/evidence cells.
- Planning consumes constraints and decisions.
- Execution receives only permitted representations.

## Invariants

- Authority labels survive summarization.
- Loss contracts are explicit.
- A derived cell points back to source bindings.

## Failure modes

- Summary overconfidence.
- Provenance loss.
- Authority escalation via compression.

## Minimal implementation

A semantic-page schema with certificate fields and example source-to-summary derivation.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Certificate completeness test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Authority preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Summary fidelity test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `vcm_public` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1 | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Public VCM release. Governed protocol for compiled working context. |
| `verification_bandwidth` | Verification Bandwidth in Bounded Contexts | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Generation vs verification; long context is not automatically long-range reasoning. |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. |
| `context_engineer` | Context Engineer / Manhattan Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Context as supply chain; compartmentalized information logistics. |
| `vcm_editable` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1.0_Editable | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Editable version with evidence-carrying planner-guided context compiler framing. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
